summary: Deep regression pass on the pricing_engine.py full rewrite (9 categories) + order_schemas.py progressive-disclosure fields + new 3-option method selectors. Backend: full pytest suite 135/141 passing (same 6 pre-existing unrelated failures confirmed, matches main agent's note), 17/17 golden pricing_engine tests pass, new parametrized API test file covering all 9 categories via /api/order-items/{id}/calculate-pricing all return 200 with positive selling_price_minor + valid calculation_method + breakdown dict, apparel quantity-derivation from size_* fields verified (size_m=12, top-level quantity=1 -> derived quantity=12). Frontend: progressive disclosure verified correct for banners (Grommets=custom -> Custom Grommet Count appears/hides) and vehicle_wrap (Custom Coverage % on coverage_type=custom, Install Difficulty/Seam Complexity/Second Installer Required hide when Install Required=off). FOUND A REAL BUG: vehicle_wrap's 'Design Complexity' field does NOT hide when 'Artwork Already Ready' is toggled ON (missing depends_on in order_schemas.py, unlike banners/apparel which correctly have it) -- contradicts the explicit spec requirement. Also FOUND A CRITICAL REGRESSION: the Order Item 'Override Price' panel (Reason input + Save Override button) overflows its card's boundary in the multi-column Orders grid layout and gets visually/functionally covered by the next sibling item card -- clicks on the Save Override button land on the sibling card instead (confirmed via document.elementFromPoint for both a first-column item 'banners' and a middle-column item 'services'; no network request fires, no override badge appears). This makes Override Price effectively unusable in Orders whenever more than one item exists per row. The equivalent Override Price flow in the single-column QuotesWorkspace works correctly (verified end-to-end, badge appeared, price updated). All 9 categories' Calculate button + pricing-breakdown UI verified working in Orders; Settings > Pricing Foundation vehicle_graphics and services 3-option method selectors verified clickable/selectable and persisted correctly via Save All (confirmed via GET /api/pricing-foundation). Quote->approve->convert-to-order->generate-invoice regression (18/18 pytest) still passing.

ui_bugs.issue: Override Price panel (Reason input + Save Override button) overflows the item card's boundary in the multi-column Order Items grid and is visually/functionally covered by the next sibling item card, so clicking 'Save Override' does not register (click lands on sibling card, no API request fires, no override badge appears, price unchanged). Reproduced for both a first-column item (banners) and a mid-row item (services). This breaks the override flow entirely whenever an order has more than one item rendered in the same grid row (i.e. almost always).

ui_bugs.component: order_schemas.py vehicle_wrap schema / OrdersWorkspace spec editor
ui_bugs.issue: vehicle_wrap's 'Design Complexity' field is missing the depends_on rule that banners/apparel have on the same field. Per explicit spec requirement, toggling 'Artwork Already Ready' ON should hide 'Design Complexity' for vehicle_wrap, but it remains visible always.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix CSS/layout so the Override Price panel (specifically the Reason input + Save Override button) does not overflow beyond its own order-item card into the sibling card's clickable area in the multi-column Orders grid. Likely needs the override panel to render at full row width, use z-index/position adjustments, or the grid to reflow when a panel is expanded.
Add depends_on={'field': 'artwork_ready', 'equals': False} to the vehicle_wrap 'design_complexity' field in order_schemas.py to match banners/apparel behavior and the explicit spec requirement.
Re-verify Override Price flow in Orders after the CSS fix, across at least 2 categories, at 1920x1080.
critical_code_review_comments: pricing_engine.py: all 9 category adapters verified functionally correct via golden tests + live API calls; well-organized single PricingResult contract, consistent warnings/breakdown shape across categories.

order_schemas.py: depends_on convention is applied consistently across banners/rigid_signs/cut_vinyl/digital_print/apparel for the artwork_ready->design_complexity pattern, but vehicle_wrap was missed (single-line omission, easy fix) -- likely a copy/paste inconsistency since vehicle_wrap's field list wasn't built from COMMON_FIELDS like the other categories.

Spec editor fields (OrdersWorkspace.js order-spec-editor) have no data-testid attributes, relying only on label text for selection -- reduces automated-test robustness for this section specifically (all other pricing controls have solid data-testid coverage).

The override panel overflow bug appears to be a pre-existing structural issue with the Orders grid (CSS Grid/Flexbox column sizing not accounting for expanded panel content width) rather than something newly introduced by this turn's engine rewrite -- but it was not caught in iteration_3 (which only tested a single order item, likely rendered alone without a sibling to overlap into).

success_rate.backend: 135/141 (95.7%, 6 pre-existing unrelated failures) + 100% on new all-categories pricing test suite (10/10)
success_rate.frontend: Progressive disclosure 3/4 verified correct (1 missing depends_on bug), Calculate+breakdown 9/9 categories working, Override Price broken in Orders (CRITICAL layout bug), Override Price working in Quotes, Pricing Foundation method selectors (vehicle_graphics + services) working and persisting

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_AllCategoriesOrder (ORD-0091) via new pytest file with 10 items (all 9 categories + 1 apparel size-derived item), left in place per convention. Created TEST_ vinyl quote line item on existing QUO-0112 via UI testing (calculated + overridden to $25.00). Changed Pricing Foundation vehicle_graphics.calculation_method to 'package_benchmark' and services.calculation_method to 'pass_through_plus_markup' via UI Save All -- real settings change, flagging in case main agent expects specific defaults.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pricing engine formulas themselves are solid across all 9 categories (backend layer fully verified). Two concrete, scoped bugs found this session: (1) CRITICAL - Override Price submit button unclickable in Orders due to card overflow/overlap in the multi-column grid (verify fix by checking document.elementFromPoint at the button's bounding-box center equals the button itself, not a sibling article), (2) MEDIUM - vehicle_wrap Design Complexity field missing depends_on artwork_ready. Both are small, targeted fixes (one CSS layout fix, one one-line schema fix). Everything else (9-category calculate+breakdown, method selectors, quote override, quote->order->invoice regression) passed cleanly.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (override panel overlap): OrdersWorkspace.js renders order items in what appears to be a multi-column CSS grid/flex layout; when 'Override Price' is toggled open, the panel's inputs+button extend past the card's own column width and are stacked below the sibling card in DOM paint order, so the sibling card's larger/later-painted box captures the click at that screen position. Reproduced consistently via document.elementFromPoint at the Save Override button's bounding box center returning the sibling item's article element instead. Fix: constrain the override panel to its own card's width (add explicit width/overflow handling, or make the panel position:absolute/full-row-width when open) so it never bleeds into sibling grid cells. Bug 2 (vehicle_wrap design_complexity): order_schemas.py line ~102 field('design_complexity', ...) omits the depends_on parameter that the equivalent field has for banners (line 22) and apparel (line 146); simply add depends_on={'field': 'artwork_ready', 'equals': False} to match.
